findings.summary: The client-side receive path accepts any server_auth message without verifying transcript_mac. An attacker can send an arbitrary identity and an all-zero MAC, after which the client records the attacker-controlled identity, transitions to AUTHENTICATED, and can complete the handshake as ESTABLISHED.
findings.evidence: Running the repro prints that a forged server_auth with transcript_mac 0000... was accepted for identity attacker.example.com even though the real MAC for the same transcript had a different prefix, and client_finish was produced after the forged authentication.

findings.summary: The server-side receive path accepts client_finish in any current state and does not verify the finished MAC. A peer can send client_finish immediately after server_hello, before server_auth is sent, with an invalid all-zero finished value; the server still transitions to ESTABLISHED.
findings.evidence: Running the repro prints that the server established before sending server_auth. The forged finished value is all zeroes and differs from the computed expected value for the same transcript, but process_message sets State.ESTABLISHED.
